entries.label: String (owned)
entries.note: Heap-allocated, growable, owned UTF-8 string

entries.label: &str (borrowed)
entries.note: Immutable reference to UTF-8 bytes; string literals are &'static str

entries.label: When to use which
entries.note: Prefer &str for params; String for ownership; impl AsRef<str> for flexibility

entries.label: Conversion
entries.note: &str -> String: .to_string(), .to_owned(), String::from(). String -> &str: &s or .as_str()

entries.label: Empty string
entries.code:
let s = String::new();
let s = String::with_capacity(100);
entries.note: with_capacity pre-allocates to avoid reallocations

entries.label: From literal
entries.code:
let s = String::from("hello");
let s = "hello".to_string();
entries.note: Most common ways to create a String

entries.label: From bytes
entries.note: from_utf8 validates; from_utf8_lossy replaces invalid bytes with U+FFFD

entries.label: From chars
entries.code:
let s: String = ['h', 'e', 'l', 'l', 'o'].iter().collect();
let s: String = "hello".chars().collect();
entries.note: Collect an iterator of chars into a String

entries.label: Repeat
entries.code:
let s = "ha".repeat(3); // "hahaha"
let s = String::from("-").repeat(40);
entries.note: Repeat a string pattern n times

entries.label: Raw string
entries.code:
let s = r"C:\Users\file"; // no escape processing
let s = r#"She said "hello""#; // delimited with #
entries.note: r"..." for raw strings; r#"..."# when content contains quotes

entries.label: Push
entries.note: push for char; push_str for &str

entries.label: Insert
entries.note: Insert at byte index; panics if not on char boundary

entries.label: Concatenation
entries.note: + takes ownership of left operand; format! creates new String without moving

entries.label: Replace
entries.code:
let s = "hello world".replace("world", "Rust");
let s = "aabaa".replacen("a", "b", 2); // "bbbaa"
entries.note: replace changes all occurrences; replacen limits count

entries.label: Remove
entries.code:
let mut s = String::from("hello");
s.truncate(3);   // "hel"
s.pop();          // Some('l'), s = "he"
s.remove(0);      // 'h', s = "e"
s.clear();        // ""
entries.note: truncate/remove work on byte indices; must be char boundaries

entries.label: Trim
entries.code:
let s = "  hello  ";
let trimmed = s.trim();       // "hello"
let left = s.trim_start();    // "hello  "
let right = s.trim_end();     // "  hello"
entries.note: Returns &str; does not modify the original

entries.label: Contains
entries.code:
"hello world".contains("world");      // true
"hello world".starts_with("hello");   // true
"hello world".ends_with("world");     // true
entries.note: Pattern can be &str, char, or &[char]

entries.label: Find position
entries.code:
"hello".find('l');    // Some(2)
"hello".rfind('l');   // Some(3)
"abcabc".find("bc");  // Some(1)
entries.note: Returns byte index of first/last match

entries.label: Check properties
entries.code:
"hello".is_empty();           // false
"".is_empty();                // true
"hello".len();                // 5 (bytes, not chars)
"hello".is_ascii();           // true
entries.note: len() returns byte count; use .chars().count() for character count

entries.label: Matches
entries.note: matches returns iterator of matches; match_indices includes positions

entries.label: Split
entries.note: split returns iterator; splitn limits number of splits

entries.label: Lines
entries.note: Handles both \n and \r\n line endings

entries.label: Join
entries.note: Join a slice of strings with a separator

entries.label: Chars and bytes
entries.note: chars() iterates Unicode scalar values; bytes() iterates raw bytes

entries.label: format! macro
entries.code:
let s = format!("Hello, {}!", name);
let s = format!("{:>10}", "right");  // right-align
let s = format!("{:<10}", "left");   // left-align
let s = format!("{:^10}", "center"); // center
entries.note: Returns a String; supports alignment and fill

entries.label: Number formatting
entries.note: Precision, padding, and base formatting

entries.label: Debug formatting
entries.note: {:?} uses Debug trait; {:#?} for pretty printing

entries.label: Named arguments
entries.note: Captured variables in format strings (since Rust 1.58)

entries.label: Padding with fill
entries.note: Fill character before alignment (< > ^), then width

entries.label: Rust strings are UTF-8
entries.note: len() is bytes, not characters; one char can be 1-4 bytes

entries.label: Char boundaries
entries.note: Indexing by byte; must land on valid char boundary or panics

entries.label: Safe character access
entries.note: Use .chars().nth() for safe character-level access

entries.label: Unicode escapes
entries.note: \u{XXXX} for Unicode code points in chars and strings

entries.label: Case conversion
entries.note: to_uppercase/lowercase handle Unicode; to_ascii_ for ASCII only

entries.label: String builder pattern
entries.note: Pre-allocate and build incrementally for efficiency

entries.label: Cow<str>
entries.note: Avoid allocation when not needed; borrows or owns as needed

entries.label: Parse from string
entries.note: parse() works for any type implementing FromStr
